title: Authenticated Scope, Secrets, SSRF, Untrusted Content, and Privacy · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for authenticated scope, secrets, ssrf, untrusted content, and privacy and accepts only compile and runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, parser, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-authority secure crawl boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Require explicit owner, account, role, purpose, paths, time, data, environment, and revocation authorization for sessions.
Acquire, scope, redact, rotate, destroy, and isolate secrets, cookies, CSRF material, caches, checkpoints, and reports per run and account.
Allow safe retrieval only, block state-changing forms, logout and destructive routes, and test unexpected GET side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Sensitive Retention
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 8203 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Sensitive Retention.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives. For Authorized site auditing and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Sensitive Retention, keep Sensitive Retention connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: Minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for least-authority secure crawl boundary.
Redacting final reports eliminates privacy risk from crawl state. That claim fails the sensitive retention · changed case evidence boundary for Sensitive Retention.

For sensitive retention · changed case, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Sensitive Retention for least-authority secure crawl boundary.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Authenticated Authority
steps.instruction:
Order the Authenticated Authority Authorized site auditing and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Authenticated Authority.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Authenticated Authority assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Authorized site auditing and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Authenticated Authority, keep Authenticated Authority connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go team handles case 5297 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to defend authenticated authority, secrets, request effects, output sinks, retention, and derived privacy state; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require explicit owner, account, role, purpose, paths, time, data, environment, and revocation authorization for sessions.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Minimize, classify, encrypt where required, restrict, expire, delete, and audit sensitive values across all derivatives.
